const greeting = (name) => `Hello, ${name}!`;
console.log(greeting('World'));

const add = (a, b) => a + b;
const multiply = (a, b) => a * b;

numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squared = [n ** 2 for n in numbers]
print(squared)

SELECT name, email
FROM users
WHERE active = true
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT 10;

INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at)
VALUES ('Alice', 'alice@example.com', NOW());

SELECT u.name, COUNT(o.id) as order_count
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
GROUP BY u.name
HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 5
ORDER BY order_count DESC;

def add(a, b):
    return a + b

result = add(3, 4)
print(result)

name = 'Alice'
age = 25
print(f'{name} is {age} years old')

def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 0

print(is_even(4))
print(is_even(7))

numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
total = sum(numbers)
print(total)

UPDATE users SET active = false
WHERE last_login < '2024-01-01';

DELETE FROM logs
WHERE created_at < '2023-01-01';

SELECT DISTINCT category
FROM products
WHERE price > 100
ORDER BY category;

const [name, setName] = useState('');
const [email, setEmail] = useState('');

Hello

Welcome to my app

SELECT 
  category,
  AVG(price) as avg_price,
  COUNT(*) as product_count
FROM products
GROUP BY category
HAVING AVG(price) > 50
ORDER BY avg_price DESC;

WITH RECURSIVE category_tree AS (
  SELECT id, name, parent_id
  FROM categories
  WHERE parent_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT c.id, c.name, c.parent_id
  FROM categories c
  JOIN category_tree ct ON c.parent_id = ct.id
)
SELECT * FROM category_tree;

SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as month,
  COUNT(*) as orders,
  SUM(total) as revenue
FROM orders
WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at)
ORDER BY month;

SELECT 
  u.name,
  u.email,
  COUNT(o.id) as total_orders,
  SUM(o.amount) as total_spent
FROM users u
JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
WHERE o.created_at >= '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY u.id, u.name, u.email
HAVING SUM(o.amount) > 1000;

def decorator_example(func):
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        print(f"Calling {func.__name__}")
        result = func(*args, **kwargs)
        print(f"Called {func.__name__}")
        return result
    return wrapper

SELECT 
  s.name as student_name,
  AVG(g.grade) as average_grade,
  CASE 
    WHEN AVG(g.grade) >= 90 THEN 'A'
    WHEN AVG(g.grade) >= 80 THEN 'B'
    WHEN AVG(g.grade) >= 70 THEN 'C'
    ELSE 'F'
  END as letter_grade
FROM students s
JOIN grades g ON s.id = g.student_id
GROUP BY s.id, s.name;

SELECT 
  p.product_name,
  p.price,
  c.category_name,
  RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_name ORDER BY p.price DESC) as price_rank
FROM products p
JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id;

SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('day', created_at) as day,
  COUNT(*) as daily_orders,
  SUM(amount) as daily_revenue,
  AVG(amount) as avg_order_value
FROM orders
WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('day', created_at)
ORDER BY day;

SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as signup_date,
  COUNT(*) as signups,
  SUM(CASE WHEN verified = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as verified,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN verified = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) as verification_rate
FROM users
WHERE created_at >= '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY DATE(created_at)
ORDER BY signup_date;

WITH monthly_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as month,
    SUM(amount) as revenue
  FROM orders
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT 
  month,
  revenue,
  LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) as prev_month,
  revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) as growth
FROM monthly_revenue;
